title: Synchronization and visual information
wikitext:
[]: Synchronization and visual information [SPOILERS!]             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sun Mar 14, 2010 10:47 am Recent swashbuckling developments within the Rebuild-verse have led me to ponder about the importance of proper eyesight when piloting an Eva. After giving the matter a few minutes of thought, i came to the conclusion that the Eva pilots don't actually need their own eyes when inside an Eva, but rather, they receive visual input directly from the Eva itself through synchronization (i can imagine that this was probably suggested before, but what the heck). Essentially, what the Eva sees get projected into the pilots brain, and the classic visual filed we can see inside the entry plug is just an added aid: a sort of HUD system . To support this theory, i would like ponit out two things: 1. The "psychedelic light show" that goes on before the pilot gets properly sycnhed up to his or her Eva. Looking at it, it's not so randomly psychedelic at all. As the pilots and the Evas nervous system gets connected, the display around the pilot goes through certain types or layers of visual inforamtion: colors, contours, movement, etc., all up to forming the well known display. I'm pretty sure that i'm giving this way more thought than anyone at Gainax ever did, but to me, this hints at the possibility that when going through the start-up process, different parts of the brain get "checked", and thus, the corresponding visual phenomena which a certain part of the brain can handle is displayed (i don't know much about neuroantomy, but i do know that areas that are responsible for the processing of visual input are pretty spread in the brain). 2. Beast Mode and Awakening Mode These two instances are the main reasons why i'm not posting this on the discussion board, but rather here. In both cases, the visual display goes totally bananas (click for bigger): [URL=http://img28.imageshack.us/my.php?image=bm2b.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://img688.imageshack.us/my.php?image=bm1k.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://img52.imageshack.us/my.php?image=awak1.jpg] [/URL] [URL=http://img22.imageshack.us/my.php?image=awak2.jpg] [/URL] Nothing of the outside world can be seen, yet the pilots seem to know full well what is happening outside (Mari is taken aback when Zeruel launches his tentacle blades at her, for example). So, unless the pilots suddenly started to use some other sense (i wonder how Zeruel smells?) to detect the enemy, it can be assumed that they still saw what was going on outside. Granted, these are special instances, and thus may be exempt from the rules of regular piloting, but i still thinks that this theory may hold water. For not only does it provide a plausible explanation on how can Phantom F. Shikinami re-enter piloting service (besides rule of cool), but also how can Mari pilot while still going all "Jinkies! My glasses!" when crashing into Shinji (unless of course, she is pulling our legs and actually has perfect eyesight).

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Mar 14, 2010 12:26 pm It was indeed suggested before: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=4120&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=0 I'm sure someone else with better searching skills can find a bit more. Some ideas include; the entry plug projects a 360 hologram, the children see through the eva's eyes, or through a camera, or through some complicated hybrid system jobby...
